name: nsgs
title: Azure Firewall — Network Security Groups
summary: Creates NSGs with prioritized allow/deny rules (CIDRs, service tags, or ASG references) and optional subnet associations.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the NSGs.

module.name: network_security_groups
module.type: map(object({ subnet_ids = optional(list(string), []) rules = optional(map(object({ priority = number direction = string access = string protocol = string source_port_ranges = optional(list(string), ["*"]) destination_port_ranges = optional(list(string), ["*"]) source_address_prefixes = optional(list(string)) destination_address_prefixes = optional(list(string)) source_application_security_group_ids = optional(list(string), []) destination_application_security_group_ids = optional(list(string), []) description = optional(string) })), {}) }))
module.description: Map of NSGs keyed by logical name. Rules accept CIDRs, Azure service tags (e.g. "Internet", "VirtualNetwork", "AzureLoadBalancer"), or ASG IDs (use the azure/firewall/asgs module independently to create ASGs and pass its IDs here). Exactly one of address prefixes or ASG IDs per side of a rule. NSGs are stateful — no return rules needed.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical NSG name to network security group ID.

outputs.name: firewall_names
outputs.description: Map of logical NSG name to NSG resource name (needed for NIC-level associations in the consumer root).

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map of '<nsg-key>/<rule-key>' to security rule ID.

outputs.name: subnet_association_ids
outputs.description: Map of '<nsg-key>/<subnet-id>' to subnet association ID.
